21.title: Regular Movements
21.subtitle: Kicks are getting stronger and more frequent.
21.keyDevDesc: Baby digests amniotic fluid for nutrition practice.
babyDevBullets: The digestive system is maturing, practicing by swallowing amniotic fluid.
Bone marrow takes over the job of producing red blood cells.
Sleep cycles become more regular (and may not match mom's!).

symptomTable.name: Braxton Hicks Contractions
symptomTable.help: Ensure she is hydrated; dehydration causes them

emotionalScripts: I love watching your belly move when the baby kicks.
practicalTasks: Start assembling the crib and any large nursery furniture.
Research pediatricians and schedule meet-and-greets.
relationshipIdeas: Spend a quiet evening feeling the baby kick together.

bondingTasks.title: Nursery Vision
bondingTasks.text: Finalize the color scheme or theme for the baby's room.

medicalRoadmap: Routine 24-week checkup is approaching, which includes the glucose test.
takeaways: Movements are becoming a regular daily pattern.
Start getting the physical nursery space ready.

22.title: Looking Like a Newborn
22.subtitle: Facial features are fully formed.
22.keyDevDesc: Baby can perceive light, sound, and touch.
22.dadPriorityDesc: Sign up for childbirth education classes.
22.partnerSymptomsDesc: Center of gravity shifts further forward.
babyDevBullets: Baby looks like a miniature newborn, just thinner.
Lips, eyelids, and eyebrows are distinct.
Tear ducts are developing.

symptomTable.name: Lower Back Pain
symptomTable.help: Offer massages, suggest a prenatal yoga class

emotionalScripts: I signed us up for the birth classes today.
practicalTasks: Register for childbirth education, breastfeeding, and newborn care classes.
Check smoke detectors and carbon monoxide monitors in the house.
relationshipIdeas: Go out for a dessert date just to talk.

bondingTasks.title: Birth Plan Chat
bondingTasks.text: Start a preliminary conversation about birth preferences.

medicalRoadmap: Prepare for the glucose screening test coming up between weeks 24-28.
takeaways: Baby's facial features are fully formed.
Education is key—book your classes now.

23.title: Hearing Fully Developed
23.subtitle: Baby is listening to your conversations.
23.partnerSymptomsDesc: Edema (water retention) begins in the lower extremities.
babyDevBullets: Bones in the middle ear harden, making hearing much sharper.
Baby can distinguish between mom's voice, your voice, and external noises.
Blood vessels in the lungs are developing to prepare for breathing air.

symptomTable.name: Swollen Ankles/Feet (Edema)
symptomTable.why: Increased blood volume and fluid retention
symptomTable.help: Elevate her feet, encourage hydration, avoid salty foods

emotionalScripts: Let me rub your feet, you've been on them all day.
practicalTasks: Review your baby budget and start buying the essentials.
Read out loud to the belly daily—baby is actively listening.
relationshipIdeas: Curate a playlist of songs you want the baby to recognize after birth.

bondingTasks.title: Story Time
bondingTasks.text: Pick a favorite children's book and read it to the bump.

medicalRoadmap: Next appointment will likely include the glucose tolerance test (GTT).
takeaways: Hearing is sharp—watch what you say!
Swelling is normal, but sudden severe swelling is a red flag (call OB).

24.title: The Viability Milestone
24.subtitle: A major milestone for survival outside the womb.
24.keyDevDesc: Baby now has a chance of survival if born prematurely.
24.partnerSymptomsDesc: Tingling or numbness in the hands.
babyDevBullets: Week 24 marks 'viability'—the point where a baby could potentially survive in the NICU.
The brain is growing rapidly, filling out the skull.
Taste buds are fully formed.

symptomTable.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
symptomTable.why: Fluid retention compressing nerves in wrists

emotionalScripts: We hit the viability milestone today. I am so relieved and happy.
practicalTasks: Take your partner to the glucose screening test (can take 1-3 hours).
Start finalizing your paternity leave paperwork with HR.
relationshipIdeas: Celebrate the milestone with a nice dinner or special treat.
optionalBuy: Wrist braces if she has carpal tunnel
medicalRoadmap: Glucose screening test for gestational diabetes.
questions: What are the signs of preterm labor we should watch for now?
takeaways: Viability is a huge emotional relief.
The glucose test is not fun, be supportive.

25.title: Recognizing Voices
25.subtitle: Baby knows who you are.
25.keyDevDesc: Wrinkled skin is smoothing out as fat builds up.
25.dadPriorityDesc: Start looking into hospital tours and preregistration.
25.partnerSymptomsDesc: Uncomfortable but common due to pressure and constipation.
babyDevBullets: Baby is adding baby fat, making the skin look less translucent and wrinkled.
Capillaries are forming under the skin, giving it a pinkish hue.
Baby responds to familiar voices, especially yours and mom's.

symptomTable.name: Hemorrhoids
symptomTable.why: Uterine pressure and increased blood flow
symptomTable.help: Buy witch hazel pads (Tucks), ensure she eats high-fiber foods

emotionalScripts: I'm here to run out to the pharmacy whenever you need.
practicalTasks: Call the hospital or birth center to ask about preregistration and tours.
Help increase her fiber intake (fruits, veggies, whole grains).
relationshipIdeas: Cook a high-fiber, healthy, and delicious meal together.

bondingTasks.title: Sing a Song
bondingTasks.text: Pick a lullaby and sing it to the baby every night. They will remember it.

medicalRoadmap: Follow up on glucose test results.
takeaways: Baby is plumping up.
Hemorrhoids are common—don't make it awkward, just buy the supplies.

26.title: Eyes Opening
26.subtitle: Baby takes a look around.
babyDevBullets: Eyelids, which have been fused shut, are now opening.
Brain wave activity for visual and auditory systems kicks in.
Baby is inhaling and exhaling small amounts of amniotic fluid to practice breathing.

symptomTable.name: Pregnancy Brain
symptomTable.help: Take over mental load tasks (scheduling, paying bills)

emotionalScripts: Don't worry about forgetting that, I've got it handled.
practicalTasks: Assist with baby shower logistics (invites, food, setup).
Take over paying the bills and managing the calendar to relieve her mental load.
relationshipIdeas: Leave a surprise note for her in her purse or car.

bondingTasks.title: Eye Contact
bondingTasks.text: The baby's eyes are open—shine a light on the belly and see if they kick!

medicalRoadmap: Discuss the Tdap (whooping cough) vaccine for both parents (usually administered between weeks 27-36).
questions: When should I (dad) get the Tdap booster?
takeaways: Baby's eyes are open.
Pregnancy brain is real—step up your organizational game.

27.title: End of Second Trimester
27.subtitle: The final stretch approaches.
27.partnerSymptomsDesc: Sharp pain shooting down the lower back and leg.
babyDevBullets: Baby's brain is growing rapidly and becoming more complex.
Lungs are continuing to mature, producing more surfactant.
Baby is sleeping and waking on a regular schedule.

symptomTable.name: Sciatica Pain
symptomTable.why: Uterus resting on the sciatic nerve
symptomTable.help: Suggest a warm bath, heating pad, or prenatal massage

emotionalScripts: I am so proud of how well you're handling all these changes.
practicalTasks: Attend childbirth classes together. Take notes and ask questions.
Book a prenatal massage for her to help with back/sciatica pain.
relationshipIdeas: Discuss your fears and hopes about labor and delivery.

bondingTasks.title: Class Notes
bondingTasks.text: Review what you learned in childbirth class together.

medicalRoadmap: End of the second trimester. Appointments will soon shift to every two weeks.
takeaways: The second trimester is over.
Take the childbirth classes seriously—you are the coach.

28.title: Third Trimester Begins
28.subtitle: The final countdown.
28.keyDevDesc: REM sleep begins; baby is likely dreaming.
28.partnerSymptomsDesc: Trouble sleeping due to size, heartburn, and anxiety.
babyDevBullets: Welcome to the third trimester!
Baby is experiencing Rapid Eye Movement (REM) sleep, meaning they are likely dreaming.
Eyes can blink and lashes are fully grown.

symptomTable.name: Insomnia
symptomTable.help: Keep the bedroom cool, avoid screens before bed

emotionalScripts: We're in the home stretch now. We can do this.
practicalTasks: Sit down and draft a one-page birth preferences document (birth plan).
Make sure you know the route to the hospital and parking situation.
relationshipIdeas: Review the birth plan over a quiet breakfast.

bondingTasks.title: Birth Plan
bondingTasks.text: Finalize your roles for labor day.

medicalRoadmap: If mom is Rh-negative, she will receive the RhoGAM shot this week.
questions: Does mom need the RhoGAM shot?
takeaways: Third trimester brings back some fatigue.
The birth plan is a communication tool, not a strict contract.

29.title: Rapid Brain Growth
29.subtitle: Brain is folding to create more surface area.
29.keyDevDesc: Smooth brain surface develops grooves and ridges.
babyDevBullets: The brain is growing so fast it's creating folds (gyri and sulci) to fit inside the skull.
Baby is packing on fat, helping regulate body temperature.
Bones are fully developed but still soft and pliable.

symptomTable.name: Constipation
symptomTable.help: Ensure she's drinking tons of water and eating fiber

emotionalScripts: How are the kicks feeling today? Nice and strong?
practicalTasks: Learn how to do 'kick counts' (timing how long it takes to feel 10 movements).
Preregister at the hospital to skip paperwork on labor day.
relationshipIdeas: Do a kick counting session together in the evening.

bondingTasks.title: Kick Counts
bondingTasks.text: Set a timer and feel for kicks together.

medicalRoadmap: Monitor kicks daily. Decreased movement is a red flag.
questions: What is your office's protocol if we notice decreased fetal movement?
takeaways: Baby's brain is highly active.
Kick counting is a vital daily task from now on.

30.title: Bone Marrow Taking Over
30.subtitle: The body systems are refining.
30.keyDevDesc: Bone marrow completely takes over RBC production.
30.dadPriorityDesc: Buy it, read the manual, install it.
30.partnerSymptomsDesc: Uterus is pushing up against the diaphragm.
babyDevBullets: Bone marrow assumes the production of red blood cells from the spleen.
Amniotic fluid begins to decrease as baby takes up more room.
Baby is getting stronger, and kicks might actually hurt mom a bit.

symptomTable.name: Breathlessness
symptomTable.help: Remind her to take it slow; don't let her carry anything

emotionalScripts: Take your time, there is no rush.
practicalTasks: Install the infant car seat base in your car. Get it inspected by a certified technician.
Identify your pediatrician and ensure they accept your insurance.
relationshipIdeas: Celebrate finishing the major nursery tasks.

bondingTasks.title: Car Seat Check
bondingTasks.text: Install the car seat and test snapping the carrier in and out.

mustBuy: Infant Car Seat (do not leave the hospital without it)
takeaways: Space is getting tight in there.
The car seat is non-negotiable. Install it now.
